channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Edmonton Valley Zoo’s Nocturnal Wing Reopens After Renovations
item.description: The Edmonton Valley Zoo’s nocturnal wing, home to a colony of 92 Jamaican fruit bats, officially reopened to the public on July 4, 2026, following extensive renovations. The upgrades included a new ba...

item.title: Australia's Socceroos eliminated by Egypt in World Cup clash
item.description: Egypt's Pharaohs ended Australia's FIFA World Cup campaign with a penalty shootout victory in Dallas. The match, which saw the Socceroos face elimination after a hard-fought encounter, highlighted bot...

item.title: Jaswant Singh Khalra's Fight Against Disappearances and His Own Vanishing
item.description: The article chronicles the tragic story of Jaswant Singh Khalra, a human rights activist who dedicated his life to exposing the systematic disappearances of young men from Punjab. His relentless effor...

item.title: Lesotho's Winter Wonderland: A Budget-Friendly Snow Destination for South Africans
item.description: Lesotho, known as the Kingdom in the Sky, offers a unique winter experience for South Africans seeking affordable snow adventures without the high costs of European destinations. Between June and Augu...

item.title: FG adopts Adire fabric for NYSC uniforms to boost local production
item.description: The Federal Government has announced plans to replace the traditional khaki uniforms of the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) with locally produced Adire fabric. This move aims to promote Nigerian c...

item.title: Bam Margera to Watch 'Jackass: Best & Last' Despite No Reunion Plans
item.description: Bam Margera, the former Jackass star, has stated he plans to watch the final film in the Jackass franchise, 'Jackass: Best & Last,' but confirmed a reunion with his former co-stars is 'not going to ha...

item.title: Mornington Peninsula Weather Forecast for Today
item.description: The article discusses the weather forecast for the Mornington Peninsula, providing details on expected conditions. However, the text provided appears to be a message from News Corp Australia regarding...

item.title: Socceroos and Egypt draw in World Cup match as Hany scores two own-goals
item.description: In a dramatic World Cup encounter, Egypt's Mohamed Hany became the first player in tournament history to score two own-goals after a controversial incident involving Australia's Aiden O'Neill. The mat...

item.title: Human Rights Groups Demand Repeal of Anti-Terrorism Laws After Six Years
item.description: Human rights groups have once again urged the repeal of the Terrorism Financing Prevention and Suppression Act of 2012 and the Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020, six years after their enactment. During a ral...

item.title: Education, political ideology, and societal divides in Spain
item.description: The article explores the complex relationship between Spain's education system and political polarization, highlighting how ideological battles over curriculum and teaching methods have deepened socie...

item.title: Tanzania's Export Earnings Reach $17 Billion in 2025
item.description: Tanzania has recorded significant growth in its export earnings, reaching nearly $17 billion in 2025. This milestone was announced by Minister Judith Kapinga at the 50th Dar es Salaam International Tr...

item.title: Technical error message regarding crawler bot access restrictions
item.description: The article contains an error message indicating that the user has been blocked as a crawler bot by News Corp Australia's website security measures. The message explains that if the user is not a bot ...

item.title: Ramaphosa seeks court review to delay impeachment process
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa's legal team has argued that a public hearing to assess the validity of the panel report underpinning his impeachment would be unjust. In an urgent application to the Western...

item.title: Congress Calls for Disbanding Ram Temple Trust Over Donation Theft
item.description: The Indian National Congress has formally demanded the dissolution of the Ram temple trust, citing allegations of corruption and mismanagement in the handling of donations. The party's leader, Jairam ...

item.title: Lionel Messi's enduring dominance: The role of perception and scanning in football
item.description: Lionel Messi, at 39 years old, defies conventional notions of athletic greatness by relying on perception and scanning rather than physical attributes like speed or height. Despite being shorter and s...

item.title: Canadian Country Star Dies at 89, Known for Long-Running TV Show
item.description: Tommy Hunter, a Canadian country music icon, passed away at the age of 89. He was renowned for his work as a television variety show host, where he entertained audiences for decades. His career spanne...

item.title: Maternal sexual abuse and the challenges in child protection systems
item.description: The article highlights the underreported issue of maternal sexual abuse and exploitation of children, emphasizing the societal denial and systemic challenges in addressing this form of child abuse. It...

item.title: Tanzanian Police Arrest 239 Suspects in Murder and Rape Cases
item.description: Tanzanian police have arrested 239 suspects in connection with serious crimes including murder and rape, as part of a nationwide crackdown on violent offenses. The operation, coordinated across multip...

item.title: Retiring Postie's Kindness Highlights Civilisation's Foundations
item.description: The article explores how a retired postal worker's acts of kindness illustrate the role of compassion in shaping society. It begins with a column by Tim the Yowie Man, who reflects on the importance o...

item.title: INEC Extends Voter Registration Deadline to July 24
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has officially extended the nationwide Continuous Voter Registration (CVR) exercise by two weeks, pushing the deadline from July 10 to July 24, 202...
